# Architecture

## Project identity decoupled from Git metadata

[Trigger]: When initializing a new project or checking its identity. [Detail]: The system derives the project's unique identity from a hash of the directory contents rather than relying on Git commit hashes, branch names, or remote URLs. Consequence: Projects can be moved, renamed, or cloned without breaking their identity linkage, but this means the project ID is not portable across different Git repositories containing the same codebase.

_Source: `src/lib/projects.js`_

_Tags: `database`, `backend`_

## Project identity derived from directory hash, not git metadata

[Trigger]: When initializing a new project or resolving project identity. [Detail]: The system computes a unique identifier using a hash of the directory contents rather than relying on `.git` metadata, commit hashes, or remote URLs. This allows projects to be portable across different repositories or detached states. [Consequence]: Projects can be cloned from any source without requiring git history, but changes to file content immediately alter the project ID, potentially breaking associations with existing knowledge entries if not handled via versioned snapshots.

_Source: `src/knowledge/vault.js`_

_Tags: `database`, `config`_
